findings.title: Inconsistent logout label in desktop vs. mobile nav
findings.description: The desktop user dropdown showed 'Logout' (one word) while the mobile drawer used 'Log out' (two words). Both authentication triggers should use the same label.
findings.fix: Changed desktop dropdown 'Logout' to 'Log out' to match mobile drawer.

findings.title: Login page heading uses 'Login' instead of 'Sign in'
findings.description: The login page <h1> read 'Login to Ready' while the submit button and the link on the signup page both use 'Sign In'. All sign-in entry points should use consistent terminology.
findings.fix: Changed heading from 'Login to Ready' to 'Sign in to Ready'.

findings.title: Coaching form inputs missing accessible label association
findings.description: Form inputs in NegotiationCoach and RejectionCoaching had visible <label> elements but no htmlFor/id linkage. Screen readers could not announce which label belongs to which field.
findings.fix: Added htmlFor on all <label> elements and matching id attributes on all <input>/<textarea> elements.

findings.title: Build failures preventing app from shipping
findings.description: Multiple TypeScript compilation errors prevented production builds: unused React imports, incorrect type-only import syntax (verbatimModuleSyntax), parameter property declarations disallowed by erasableSyntaxOnly, wrong AI task names ('skills-gap' vs 'analyze-skills-gap', 'interview-prep' vs 'prepare-interview'), wrong DB column name ('interview_prep_id' vs 'practice_prep_id'), and JSX.Element namespace not found.
findings.fix: Fixed all 26 TypeScript compilation errors. Build now succeeds.

findings.title: Stub pages expose incomplete UI to users
findings.description: Three pages show only a heading and one sentence: /mirror/linkedin (LinkedIn Analysis), /mirror/portfolio (Portfolio Analysis), and /settings (Settings). Users landing on these routes see no loading state, no empty state, no meaningful actions, and no indication that the feature is coming.
findings.recommendation: Either implement the feature or replace the stub with a proper 'Coming Soon' empty state component using the existing EmptyState UI component with a descriptive CTA.

findings.title: No error boundaries around feature sections
findings.description: The app has an ErrorBoundary component but it is only used at the top level in App.tsx (inferred). Individual page sections (Practice, Skills Gap, Coaching, Playback) have no per-section error boundaries. A single component crash will replace the entire page with the error fallback instead of degrading gracefully.
findings.recommendation: Wrap each major route section with an ErrorBoundary to allow partial-page degradation.

findings.title: Cloudinary hero and texture images have no alt text
findings.description: The theme system exposes heroImage and textureImage URLs via context. Any component rendering will produce an with no alt attribute, which is a WCAG 2.1 Level A failure.
findings.recommendation: When rendering hero/texture images, always include alt='' (decorative) or a descriptive alt string, e.g. alt='Interview readiness hero illustration'.

findings.title: You're Ready page navigation ambiguity
findings.description: The /youre-ready celebration page is accessible via a direct route but is not linked from the main navigation. Users who navigate away cannot easily return to it. The trigger condition (80%+ readiness) is also not clearly communicated.
findings.recommendation: Either add a breadcrumb/back button or surface the milestone in the Playback or Dashboard pages so users can re-access the celebration summary.

findings.title: Product voice guide undefined
findings.description: The app uses a mix of motivational ('Your story awaits'), instructional ('Paste the rejection email'), and casual ('Get unstuck') tones without a documented voice guide. This makes it difficult to audit copy for consistency or onboard new writers.
findings.recommendation: Define a product voice guide (e.g. 'Encouraging but direct. Second person. Action-first verbs.') and store it in docs/voice-guide.md.
